description: Evaluator-only known Web conflicts for the optional C-stage Web snapshot extension. Do not provide this file to AI tools under evaluation.

conflicts.title: Page flow differs from documented business sequence
conflicts.description: Web navigation may combine shortcuts such as PR detail to contract or invoice workbench links, while policy documents describe a stricter sequential business process.
conflicts.expected_handling: Record as candidate page-flow evidence and do not claim it is the only or canonical process order.
conflicts.review_notes: Useful for testing whether tools distinguish navigation shortcuts from business rules.

conflicts.title: Visible Web buttons do not prove backend capability
conflicts.description: Buttons such as Activate Contract, Release Hold, Return, Match Invoice and Mark Paid are UI evidence only unless confirmed by code, DDL or documents.
conflicts.expected_handling: Keep as candidate actions with uncertainty until fused with non-Web sources.
conflicts.review_notes: This is the main hallucination-control boundary for Web UI analysis.

conflicts.title: UI labels differ from DDL/code naming
conflicts.description: UI labels include PR No, Preferred Vendor, Vendor, GRN and Payment Application, while database and other sources may use request_no, Supplier, goods_receipt and payment_request.
conflicts.expected_handling: Create aliases and mappings rather than duplicate concepts.
conflicts.review_notes: Expected to test alias handling across UI, documents and database sources.

conflicts.title: UI visibility hints are not final permission rules
conflicts.description: Page role hints and visible buttons can differ from the role permission matrix and must not be treated as final authorization rules without fusion.
conflicts.expected_handling: Represent as role visibility evidence or permission candidates with uncertainty.

conflicts.title: Some implied business objects have no standalone page snapshot
conflicts.description: Supplier Master, Purchase Order Detail and Goods Receipt Detail are intentionally absent or only represented through related pages.
conflicts.expected_handling: Record missing pages and do not invent unseen fields or flows.
conflicts.review_notes: This guards against invented recursive crawling results.

conflicts.title: Some UI terms are Web-only or operational candidates
conflicts.description: Release Hold, Payment Application No and Hold Reason appear as UI terms and should remain candidates unless confirmed elsewhere.
conflicts.expected_handling: Represent as Web-only candidates or uncertainties; do not add required database fields without evidence.
conflicts.review_notes: Especially important for payment hold reason and release-hold action handling.
